reviewer: build-wave agent (real headless-Chrome first-visit download → streamed inference + cached-reload auto-init verification, revision 1)

rubric.evidence: Real first-visit run in headless Chrome (WASM, no-GPU): loader state 'download-required' → clicked 'Download model (~460 MB)' → reached state=ready → clicked Summarize on the science sample → the summary STREAMED in and settled to 'The planet, which has roughly the mass of Neptune but a size closer to Jupiter, orbits its star every six days. It is one of the puffiest, lowest-density worlds known. The findings mark one of most detailed looks yet at the atmosphere of a…' — a genuine abstractive summary. Standalone transformers.js@3.7.5 (q8) check produced a coherent WASP-107b summary earlier. Console + network clean (0 errors, 0 failures). Screenshot: /home/paulkinlan/tmp/verify-new/bart-large-cnn-summarization-desktop.png.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in models/bart-large-cnn-summarization/worker.js; TextStreamer streams tokens and timestamps each for the real decode timeline.

rubric.evidence: Output is a single abstractive summary string ([{summary_text}]) that faithfully compresses the input in fresh sentences — verified against the science, business and history samples plus the standalone exoplanet article. The See-inside panel reports real input/output token counts and the compression ratio derived from the model's own tokenizer.
rubric.notes: Length capped to ~90% of input tokens to stop the model padding/repeating on short inputs (honest summaries).

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance table (desktop screenshot) shows the exact model id Xenova/bart-large-cnn linked to its card, task summarization (seq-to-seq), backend WebAssembly, quantisation q8, ~460 MB (encoder + decoder) with an explicit contrast to DistilBART's ~250 MB, MIT. conformance: declares-model-id / declares-task / declares-backend / declares-quantisation / inference-off-main-thread all derive-pass.
rubric.notes: Worker imports TRANSFORMERS_URL (3.7.5) and runs in a Web Worker; device 'wasm'.

rubric.evidence: Cached-reload test (same profile, Cache Storage persists): the page AUTO-initialised to state=ready with NO Download click; the only action button shown was 'Clear cached model' (Download button present: false). Verified programmatically and by screenshot /home/paulkinlan/tmp/verify-new/bart-large-cnn-summarization-cached.png. The ~460 MB model is never silently re-downloaded — the loader validates the cached copy and auto-inits it.
rubric.notes: Uses the shared lib/model-loader.js createModelLoader auto-init architecture; no hand-rolled Load button.

rubric.evidence: live-status-region=pass (role=status aria-live on #runStatus and the loader status), errors-on-the-page=pass (summarization failures write to an .err status). Console clean on first visit AND cached reload (0 errors). runs-without-webgpu-fallback: WASM-only model — it ran fully on the no-GPU runner. Download progress surfaced via the shared loader <progress> across a 460 MB transfer without stalling the UI.
rubric.notes: Retry path provided by createModelLoader on failure; summary streams token-by-token so the user sees progress mid-generation.

rubric.evidence: Main page: source textarea (labelled), 3 sample chips, Max/Min length + Beams range sliders with live value read-outs, Paragraph/Bullets segmented toggle (aria-pressed), Summarize button, loader Download/Clear. Basics: length range. Practical: paragraph/bullets + beams. Wild: 'Distill to one sentence' recursive chain. Multi-model: two loaders, length range, voice <select>, Summarize + Read-aloud. All real, labelled, keyboard-operable. buttons-have-names=pass.
rubric.notes: Bullets mode reshapes the generated paragraph into sentences after the fact — honestly labelled, not a separate model claim.

rubric.evidence: Every visible summary comes from the on-device seq-to-seq run; verified by watching the streamed output form live and differ per input/length. Sample source texts are inputs, never canned outputs; no hardcoded summary strings in the source.

images-have-alt=pass (the decode-timeline <canvas> carries an aria-label describing the chart; no content images), buttons-have-names=pass. Segmented toggle uses aria-pressed; sliders have labelled live values; output region is aria-live. Light + dark both legible: /home/paulkinlan/tmp/verify-new/bart-large-cnn-summarization-{desktop,dark}.png.
rubric.notes: Design-system tokens throughout; the timeline canvas re-reads theme colours on draw so it stays legible in both themes.

rubric.evidence: responsive-check (automated): desktop (1280×800) and mobile (360×740 DPR3) both clean — no horizontal overflow, no clipped controls, console+network clean. Screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/bart-large-cnn-summarization/{desktop,mobile}.png. The sum-io and controls grids use auto-fit minmax() so the source/summary panes stack on mobile; the timeline canvas is width:100%.
rubric.notes: Logical properties throughout; multi-model loader-pair and steps stack on narrow viewports. support recorded needs-review pending a deeper agent tap-target pass.

rubric.evidence: All generation runs in a Web Worker; the main thread only appends streamed text and draws a small canvas. BART-large is heavier than DistilBART, so per-token time is higher (shown honestly in the decode timeline), but the UI never blocks — the 460 MB download + WASM compile completed with a clean console and responsive controls. No main-thread inference loop; INP stays low.
rubric.notes: Streaming keeps the user informed during the longer BART-large decode; guidance break-up-long-tasks satisfied by the worker.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel grid without horizontal overflow on mobile and desktop
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Grid repeat(auto-fit, minmax()) for side-by-side panes; flex-wrap for chip/control rows; logical properties; width:100% media/canvas; avoid fixed widths.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — sum-io (source/summary) and controls grids use auto-fit minmax() and stack on mobile; the decode-timeline canvas is inline-size:100%; multi-model loader-pair + steps stack. Verified no overflow at 360px and 1280px.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep input responsive INP break up long tasks web worker streaming
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy work off the main thread; stream progress incrementally to keep the UI responsive.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all generation runs in a Web Worker; the summary streams token-by-token via TextStreamer so the main thread only ever appends small text updates, even during the heavier BART-large decode.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; console clean during a 460 MB load + streamed generation.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible loading progress and error retry states live region segmented toggle
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce status/errors in a live region; use aria-pressed for toggle buttons; label sliders and their values.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live on the run/loader status; Paragraph/Bullets uses aria-pressed; length/beam sliders have labelled live value read-outs; output is aria-live.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible form textarea range slider controls labels
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Associate labels with textareas and range inputs; expose current values.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — textareas have wrapping <label>s; range inputs show a live 'N tokens' / beam-mode value; the multi-model voice picker is a labelled <select> with grouped <optgroup>s.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (shared design system, invariant 6).

openQuestions: Should the page offer a side-by-side BART-large-vs-DistilBART run on the same input (two models loaded) to make the quality contrast tangible, or keep the contrast textual to avoid a ~710 MB combined download?
For very long inputs beyond BART's 1024-token window, is the recursive (wild) collapse the right answer, or should the practical page auto-chunk and stitch?

followUpGoals.goal: Add a BART-large-vs-DistilBART head-to-head demo that runs both summarizers on one article and diffs the outputs + timings, so the higher-quality claim is shown, not just stated.
Add an auto-chunking practical variant that splits inputs longer than the 1024-token window, summarizes each chunk, and stitches a final pass — for full-length articles.
Deeper mobile tap-target + focus-order audit on the sliders and segmented toggle at 360px to flip support from needs-review to ok with recorded evidence.

summary: Revision-1 critique for bart-large-cnn-summarization (Xenova/bart-large-cnn, summarization, WASM q8, ~460 MB, MIT), backed by a real headless first-visit download → streamed abstractive summary of a science article, a cached-reload auto-init check (no Download button, 'Clear cached model' offered, no silent re-download of the 460 MB model), a standalone transformers.js check, conformance 14✓/0✗/9 manual, and a clean desktop+mobile responsive scan. This is the canonical full-size BART DistilBART distils from — the page names the size/speed trade-off honestly and links the distilled sibling. Strong, honest demo; a live BART-vs-DistilBART head-to-head and mobile tap-target depth are the follow-ups.
